fields.description: e.g., "Light"
fields.name: title
fields.title: Theme Name

fields.description: URL-safe version. e.g., "light"
fields.name: slug

fields.description: e.g., "What does light reveal that darkness hides?"
fields.name: question
fields.title: Central Question

fields.description: 2–3 sentence description of the theme for the Explore/Journey UI
fields.name: description

fields.description: Hex color for UI pills and theme cards. e.g., "#C19B5F"
fields.name: color

fields.description: Display order in theme lists. Initial: 1–5.
fields.name: order
fields.title: Display Order

fields.description: Optional hero image for theme cards in Explore UI
fields.name: image
fields.title: Hero Image

fields.description: What kind of content is this?
fields.name: contentType

fields.description: e.g., "Pietà" or "Into Great Silence"
fields.name: title

fields.description: The work's creator. e.g. "Michelangelo Buonarroti" for sacred-art, "Julia Margaret Cameron" for photography. Shown on the P&P as part of the museum-style caption directly below the image (for sacred-art and photography only). Also used in Explore detail attribution string.
fields.name: artist
fields.title: Artist / Creator (sacred-art + photography)

fields.description: e.g., "St. Augustine", "Roger Scruton"
fields.name: thinkerName

fields.description: e.g., "Gerard Manley Hopkins"
fields.name: author

fields.description: e.g., "Arvo Pärt", "J.S. Bach"
fields.name: composer

fields.description: Which themes this content speaks to (min 1).
fields.name: themes

fields.description: Short description for cards and previews. 1–2 sentences.
fields.name: description
fields.title: Brief Description

fields.description: The story hook, the surprising fact that opens the encounter. About the work or its subject. Safe to show anywhere this piece appears (Journey Day Encounter step, Explore detail card, Library). For sacred-art the hook can be artwork-specific or subject-story; for thinker / literature / music it is the surprising fact about the work or its subject. Journey-day-specific hooks (text that only makes sense within that journey arc) live on journeyDay.encounterNote. P&P-specific hooks live on dailyPrompt.curatorNote.
fields.name: artworkHook
fields.title: Hook (Encounter - 1st text)

fields.description: Optional MP3 narration of the hook. Adds a small listen button.
fields.name: artworkHookAudio
fields.title: Hook, Narration Audio

fields.description: Deeper context. Why this matters, what to notice, how it connects to the tradition.
fields.name: context

fields.description: Optional MP3 narration of the context field. Adds a small listen button.
fields.name: contextAudio
fields.title: Context, Narration Audio

fields.description: High-res image. Min 1200px. Every content item must have an image.
fields.name: image

fields.description: When the work was created. e.g. "1498–1499", "c. 1600", "1873" (Cameron). Shown on the P&P as part of the museum-style caption below the image (for sacred-art and photography only).
fields.name: year
fields.title: Year / Date Range (sacred-art + photography)

fields.description: The physical medium of the work. e.g. "Oil on canvas", "Marble", "Fresco" for sacred-art, "Albumen print", "Silver gelatin" for photography. Optional. Used in Explore detail attribution.
fields.name: medium
fields.title: Medium (sacred-art + photography)

fields.description: Optional scripture pairing tied to the work itself (not the same as dailyPrompt.lectio, which is per-day). Renders on the Explore detail card REFLECT expand panel.
e.g., "Lamentations 1:12"
fields.name: reference
fields.title: Scripture Reference

fields.name: scripturePairing
fields.title: Scripture Pairing (sacred-art + photography)

fields.description: The primary quote. Source = work title.
e.g., "Confessions, Book X"
fields.name: source
quote
fields.title: Primary Quote

fields.description: Historical era of the thinker
fields.name: era

fields.description: e.g., "Platonist", "Thomist", "Phenomenologist"
fields.name: tradition

fields.description: e.g., "God's Grandeur" or "The Brothers Karamazov"
fields.name: workTitle

fields.description: A passage or stanza. Must be public domain or properly licensed.
fields.name: excerpt

fields.description: e.g., "Hilliard Ensemble"
fields.name: performer

fields.description: Approximate listening/viewing time
fields.name: durationMinutes
fields.title: Duration (minutes)

fields.description: Optional audio pairing for any content type. Use the file upload for in-app playback (preferred). Use the URL for direct MP3 links. External link (YouTube, Spotify) opens outside the app.
Upload an MP3 — plays directly in KALLOS. Preferred over URL.
fields.name: audioFile
fields.title: Audio File Upload (in-app playback)

fields.description: Direct MP3 link (e.g. archive.org) — plays in-app. Use if file upload is not possible.
fields.name: audioUrl
fields.title: Audio URL (in-app playback)

fields.description: YouTube, Spotify, or Apple Music. Only used if no file or audio URL is provided. Opens outside the app.
fields.name: externalUrl
fields.title: External Link (reference only)

fields.name: audioSource

fields.description: e.g., "Cistercian monastic brewing", "3rd-generation Neapolitan bakery"
fields.name: craftTradition

fields.description: What does this point toward? The beauty-connection.
fields.name: pointsToward

fields.description: How this landscape speaks to creation theology. Paired thinker/scripture connection.
fields.name: creationTheology

fields.description: YouTube, Vimeo, or podcast URL
fields.name: mediaUrl
fields.title: Media URL

fields.description: If part of a series. e.g., "Bishop Barron's Word on Fire"
fields.name: series

fields.description: e.g., Mathematics, Physics, Biology, Astronomy, Geometry
fields.name: discipline

fields.description: The key concept, e.g., "Golden Ratio", "Fibonacci Sequence", "Double Helix"
fields.name: principle
fields.title: Principle or Discovery

fields.description: How does this reveal beauty, truth, or the divine? The contemplative thread.
fields.name: beautyConnection

fields.description: 1–3 questions for contemplation.
fields.name: reflectionQuestions

fields.description: Optional. A traditional Catholic prayer shown in the expandable "Traditional Prayer" drawer on the Pray step of Visio Divina. Leave blank to use the built-in fallback prayer.
fields.name: traditionalPrayer
fields.title: Traditional Prayer (Pray step)

fields.description: Attribution for the prayer above, e.g. "Act of Adoration, Traditional" or "St. Francis of Assisi". Shown below the prayer text.
fields.name: traditionalPrayerSource
fields.title: Traditional Prayer — Source

fields.description: Optional. Overrides the default Pray-step instruction sentence for this artwork. Leave blank to use the global Visio Divina default.
fields.name: customPrayerPrompt
fields.title: Custom Pray prompt (override)

fields.description: Optional. Overrides the default Actio italic headline ("How will you live this out?") for this artwork. Leave blank to use the global Visio Divina default.
fields.name: customActioHeadline
fields.title: Custom Actio headline (override)

fields.description: Optional. Overrides the default Actio supporting instruction for this artwork. Leave blank to use the global Visio Divina default.
fields.name: customActioInstruction
fields.title: Custom Actio instruction (override)

fields.description: Link specific tradition reflections to this content item for the Visio Divina "Go Deeper" carousel. Order here = carousel order. If empty, the carousel shows reflections matching this item's themes.

of.title: Reference to tradition reflection (church fathers, saints & popes)

fields.name: traditionReflections
fields.title: Go Deeper: Tradition Reflections

fields.description: e.g., "St. Peter's Basilica", "Grande Chartreuse"
fields.name: locationName

fields.description: e.g., "Rome", "Florence"
fields.name: city

fields.description: KALLOS is global — no default country.
fields.name: country

fields.description: For Explore map view. Only populate when content has a meaningful physical location.
fields.name: coordinates
fields.title: Map Coordinates

fields.description: Category of the source
fields.name: authorType
fields.title: Author type

fields.description: Short label, e.g. "Beauty as a path to God"
fields.name: title

fields.description: 2–4 sentences in plain language (the reflection, not necessarily a direct quote)
fields.name: summary

fields.description: Optional MP3 narration of the full reflection. Adds a small listen button when expanded.
fields.name: reflectionAudio
fields.title: Reflection — Narration Audio

fields.description: One memorable line to show above the summary, if desired
fields.name: shortQuote
fields.title: Short quote (optional)

fields.description: Attribution, e.g. "St. Augustine, On True Religion" or "Benedict XVI, Address to Artists"
fields.name: source

fields.description: Which KALLOS themes this reflection connects to. Replaces the legacy theme dropdown.
fields.name: themes

fields.description: Kept for migration. Use the Themes field above going forward.
fields.name: theme
fields.title: Theme (legacy — use Themes above)

fields.description: Lower numbers appear first when showing multiple reflections
fields.name: order
fields.title: Display order

fields.description: e.g. "Ancient", "Early Church", "Medieval", "Modern" – for future use or labels
fields.name: era
fields.title: Era (optional)

fields.description: Name of the piece, e.g. "Miserere Mei, Deus" or "Gymnopédies No. 1".
fields.name: title

fields.description: Just the name, e.g. "Gregorio Allegri" or "Arvo Pärt". Used for dedup and content-repeat analysis.
fields.name: composerArtist
fields.title: Composer / Artist

fields.description: Just the piece title, e.g. "Miserere Mei, Deus". Used for dedup — same composer twice is fine; same work twice is repetition.
fields.name: workTitle
fields.title: Work Title (structured)

fields.description: Select the closest genre. Used to track variety and prevent over-reliance on any single register.
fields.name: genre

fields.description: e.g. "Public domain — multiple recordings on Musopen and Internet Archive"
fields.name: licensingNote

fields.description: Upload an MP3 — plays directly in KALLOS.
fields.name: audioFile
fields.title: Audio File (in-app playback)

fields.description: Direct MP3 link (e.g. archive.org) — plays in-app. Use this OR the file upload, not both.
fields.name: audioUrl
fields.title: Audio URL (in-app playback)

fields.description: YouTube, Spotify, or Apple Music. Only use if no Audio File or Audio URL is provided above. Opens outside KALLOS and breaks the in-app experience.
fields.name: externalUrl
fields.title: External Link (reference only — opens outside app)

fields.description: Optional. Paste lyrics or text here in any format — Latin only, English only, or both together (interleaved or stacked). Shown in a scrollable expandable panel below the audio player, labelled VERBA.
fields.name: verbaOriginal
fields.title: Verba — Text

fields.description: Journey title. e.g., "Light"
fields.name: title

fields.description: URL-safe. Source: title.
fields.name: slug

fields.description: Which theme this Journey explores
fields.name: theme

fields.description: Controls which section this journey appears in on the Journeys page. Standard goes in "Journeys"; Intro goes in "Where to Begin" with a START HERE badge; Companion goes in "Episode Companions" with companion card treatment.
fields.name: journeyType

fields.description: Companion journeys only. The name of the show or series this journey pairs with. Example: "Seeking Beauty". Appears in the dark teal strip at the top of the companion card.
fields.name: showName

fields.description: Companion journeys only. The episode identifier shown on the companion card strip. Example: "Episode 1".
fields.name: episodeLabel

fields.description: What the seeker will explore over 7 days. Shown on Journey card.
fields.name: description

fields.description: Cover image for Journey card. High-res.
fields.name: heroImage

fields.description: Default: 10. Shown as "~10 min/day".
fields.name: estimatedMinutesPerDay

fields.description: Toggle on when all 7 days are ready.
fields.name: isPublished
fields.title: Published

fields.description: Display order in Journey list.
fields.name: order
fields.title: Display Order

fields.description: References to journeyDay documents. Add existing journeyDay docs or create new ones. Each day follows: Open → Encounter → Reflect → Connect → Go Deeper.
fields.name: days

fields.description: The intended total number of days for this journey. Used for the progress bar and day count even before all days are built. Leave blank to auto-calculate from built + planned days.
fields.name: totalDays
fields.title: Total Days (full arc)

fields.description: Day numbers and titles for days not yet built in Sanity. These appear greyed out and non-tappable in the app, so users can see the full journey arc before all days are published.
Must match the intended day slot (e.g. 2 for Day 2).
fields.name: dayNumber

fields.description: The title users will see in the greyed-out row.
fields.name: dayTitle
plannedDays
fields.title: Planned Days (coming soon)

fields.description: The journey this day belongs to.
fields.name: journey

fields.description: e.g., "The First Light" or "What Darkness Teaches"
fields.name: dayTitle

fields.description: ALWAYS required. The visual anchor for the day. Min 1200px.
fields.name: openImage
fields.title: Opening Image

fields.description: Brief opening text (~1 min read). Sets the day's frame.
fields.name: openText
fields.title: Opening Text

fields.description: Optional MP3 narration of the opening text. Adds a small listen button.
fields.name: openTextAudio
fields.title: Opening Text — Narration Audio

fields.description: The primary content for this day (3–5 min encounter).
fields.name: encounterContent

fields.description: Optional editorial guidance. e.g., "Listen with your eyes closed for the first minute."
fields.name: encounterGuidance

fields.description: Interpretive layer specific to this day's content type. Sacred art → symbolism/iconography. Literature → close reading. Music → listening guide. Landscape → ecological/geological insight. Thinkers → intellectual context. Pattern & Proof → plain-language explanation. Watch & Listen → viewing guide. Food & Wine → craft tradition.
fields.name: encounterNote
fields.title: Encounter Note (Look Closer)

fields.description: Optional MP3 narration of the Look Closer text. Adds a Listen button at the top of the Look Closer section.
fields.name: encounterNoteAudio
fields.title: Encounter Note — Narration Audio

fields.description: Optional audio pairing for this day. Create the Auditio document first, then reference it here.
fields.name: auditio
fields.title: Auditio (Audio Suggestion)

fields.description: Optional philosophy + scripture pairing for this day. Connects the encounter to broader intellectual and spiritual tradition.
A quote from a philosopher, thinker, or writer (may be secular). e.g., Plato, Bergson, Socrates.
fields.name: philosophyQuote

fields.description: Full attribution. e.g., "Plato, Republic (Allegory of the Cave)"
fields.name: philosophySource

fields.description: Scripture pairing for this day.
fields.name: scriptureVerse

fields.description: e.g., "1 Corinthians 13:12"
fields.name: scriptureReference

fields.description: Why this pairing works. How the philosophy and scripture echo each other and connect to the day's encounter.
fields.name: connectionNote
lectio

fields.description: Journey-specific questions for this day (1–2).
fields.name: reflectionQuestions

fields.description: Optional MP3 narration of the reflection questions. Adds a Listen button on the Reflect step.
fields.name: reflectionQuestionsAudio
fields.title: Reflection Questions — Narration Audio

fields.description: One sentence threading to tomorrow. e.g., "Tomorrow: what happens when the light goes away?"
fields.name: connectThread

fields.description: Optional tradition reflections for this day.

of.title: Reference to tradition reflection (church fathers, saints & popes)

fields.name: goDeeper

fields.description: Renders on the P&P as the small sage-caps date above the artwork title. Required. One prompt per day. Format YYYY-MM-DD.
fields.name: date

fields.description: The contentItem this prompt features. Drives the artwork image, title, context paragraphs, and (on the linked piece) the artworkHook (which is NOT shown on P&P, only on Journey Day Encounter). Required.
fields.name: content
fields.title: Featured Content

fields.description: Renders on the P&P as the dark hook box just below the artwork title. The most prominent USER-FACING text on the prompt screen, not internal. The P&P-specific story hook for THIS day, the surprising fact or arc that opens this day's practice. Distinct from contentItem.artworkHook (which is the universal hook for the piece on Journey Day Encounter); curatorNote is THIS day's framing of the same piece. See CLAUDE.md "Hook Rule" for the three-fields-three-jobs rule.
fields.name: curatorNote
fields.title: Curator Note (P&P, dark hook box)

fields.description: Renders on the P&P inside the dark hook box as a small listen button below the curatorNote text. Optional MP3 narration.
fields.name: curatorNoteAudio
fields.title: Curator Note, Narration Audio (P&P, dark hook box)

fields.description: Renders on the P&P as the italic Cormorant subtitle, sitting BETWEEN the curatorNote dark hook box (above) and the long-form context storytelling paragraphs (below). Optional poetic editorial title for THIS day, e.g. "God Does Not Die" for the Sacred Heart day. Falls back to promptQuestion in the same slot if blank. Distinct from contentItem.title (the work's actual name, renders as the large 50px Cormorant italic at the top of the page). Added June 7, 2026 (PP-DAYTITLE-01).
fields.name: dayTitle
fields.title: Editorial Day Title (P&P, subtitle between hook box and long story)

fields.description: Renders on the P&P in the subtitle slot below the curatorNote dark box, BUT only when dayTitle is blank (dayTitle takes priority). Also used as the share-sheet text when a user shares the prompt. The reflective question for THIS day. User-facing.
fields.name: promptQuestion
fields.title: Prompt Question (P&P, subtitle fallback + share text)

fields.description: Renders on the P&P as TWO separate sections per the June 3, 2026 split (commit 5e25e8b2): a "Voices" section for the philosophy quote (sage left rule, gold/sage eyebrow), and a "Lectio" section for the scripture (Cormorant italic, no left rule). Philosophy first, scripture second. Optional philosophy + scripture pairing; scripture alone is fine if no matching philosophy quote exists.

Renders on the P&P inside the "Voices" section above the scripture. A philosopher reaching toward a truth that scripture names. Plato, Aristotle, Marcus Aurelius, Seneca, Boethius, Heraclitus, or another verified ancient. One-liner, modern accessible translation. Leave blank for scripture-only.
fields.name: philosophyText
fields.title: Philosophy Quote (P&P, "Voices" section)

fields.description: Renders on the P&P below the philosophy quote in the "Voices" section. The author + work, e.g. "Plato, Republic, Allegory of the Cave" or "Marcus Aurelius, Meditations IV.3".
fields.name: philosophyAttribution
fields.title: Philosophy Attribution (P&P, "Voices" section)

fields.description: Renders on the P&P inside the "Lectio" section below the Voices section. The scripture passage (RSV-2CE per CLAUDE.md standard) or other primary text. Required.
fields.name: text
fields.title: Scripture / Primary Text (P&P, "Lectio" section)

fields.description: Renders on the P&P below the scripture text in the "Lectio" section. The book / chapter / verse, e.g. "1 Corinthians 13:12" or "Psalm 46:10".
fields.name: attribution
fields.title: Scripture Attribution (P&P, "Lectio" section)

fields.name: lectio
fields.title: Voices & Lectio (P&P, two stacked sections)

fields.description: Renders on the P&P as the music player below the Lectio. The audio pairing for this practice. Reference to a separate Auditio document, so create that doc first then reference it here. Optional but expected per CLAUDE.md Auditio rules.
fields.name: auditio
fields.title: Music (P&P, Auditio player)

fields.description: Renders on the P&P as the Actio checkbox(es) at the bottom of the page. One concrete gentle action to carry into the day. Must name a specific place, person, object, or time (not a disposition). If left blank, the global default from the ppDefaults singleton fills in. Per CLAUDE.md Actio specificity rule.
fields.name: actio
fields.title: Action (P&P, Actio checkbox at bottom)

fields.description: Renders on the P&P as a quiet "Related Journey" CTA link near the bottom of the prompt, below the Actio. Optional. Use when a Journey deepens this prompt thematically.
fields.name: relatedJourney
fields.title: Related Journey (P&P, "Related Journey" CTA at bottom)

fields.description: INTERNAL field, not directly rendered as visible text on the P&P. Optional theme tag used by Explore filtering and dashboard analytics. Helps users on a Journey see thematic threads in P&P content.
fields.name: theme
fields.title: Theme (internal, filter only)

fields.description: Short label for this idea. Example: "Josemaría Escrivá, Spanish embassy 9 months" or "Hildegard Day 2 (deeper)". Refine later.
fields.name: workingTitle

fields.description: The conviction the user will assemble for themselves by the end. ONE sentence. NOT the topic or subject, the thesis. Example for When Myth Became Fact: "The pre-Christian myths were grasping at something real that Christianity completed." See CONTUERI-Journey-Arc-Frameworks.html for examples. If you cannot compress this to one sentence, the idea is not sharp enough yet.
fields.name: centralIdea

fields.description: If this idea grew out of researching another planning item, link the parent here. Captures lineage. Example: the Avengers/hero-myth idea grew out of When Myth Became Fact, so it would link to that planning item.
fields.name: parentIdea
fields.title: Parent Idea (where this came from)

fields.description: Which content surface this planning item is for. Use Exploring when the surface is not yet decided.
fields.name: stream

fields.description: Move items left-to-right as they progress. Archived items are kept for history but hidden from the active plan view.
fields.name: status

fields.description: Which journey this planning item belongs to. Used by the Content Planning sidebar to group journey items. Only visible when Stream is Journey or Exploring. Set during Exploring if you already know the candidate arc.
fields.name: journeyName

list.title: The Argument (evidence accumulates to an unstated thesis)

fields.description: The arc shape this content uses. See CONTUERI-Journey-Arc-Frameworks.html for descriptions of each. Visible for Journey, P&P, and Exploring items. Leave blank if the framework is not yet decided.
fields.name: framework

fields.description: What kind of content this will be. Matches the 9 contentItem.contentType values for compatibility with the eventual seed.
fields.name: contentType

fields.description: The intended launch date for this content (the date the P&P appears, or the journey starts, etc.). Leave blank if not yet scheduled.
fields.name: targetDate

fields.description: Use when a specific date is not yet decided but the month is known. e.g. "July 2026". Ignored if Target Date is set.
fields.name: targetMonth
fields.title: Target Month (fallback)

fields.description: Optional theme tags (Light, Silence, Suffering & Beauty, etc.). Helps with calendar grouping.
fields.name: themes

fields.description: If this P&P is meant to warm users up for a specific Journey (or this Journey idea relates to existing material), link it here.
fields.name: relatedJourney

fields.description: Existing P&P days that this planning item relates to (e.g., a Journey idea whose P&P teasers are already published).
fields.name: relatedPPs
fields.title: Related Pause & Ponder days

fields.description: If this content is tied to a specific feast day, name it here. e.g. "John the Baptist — June 24", "Peter & Paul — June 29", "Benedict of Nursia — July 11". Used by the calendar view to mark feast-day-anchored slots.
fields.name: feastDayAnchor

fields.description: What is the content about? What is the surprising fact, the dinner-table-test moment, the angle that earns the slot? Capture the editorial idea here before any drafting.
fields.name: subjectNotes
fields.title: Subject Notes (the lesser-known angle)

fields.description: Citations for verification: book editions, page numbers, Lectio quote provenance. For clickable research leads (podcasts, articles, Pinterest, source artwork), use Inspiration Links below.
fields.name: sourceMaterial
fields.title: Source Material (citations)

fields.description: Research leads and source material that lives on the web: podcast episodes, articles, YouTube/video, Pinterest pins, candidate artwork pages. Each link has a kind, optional label, and a note for why it matters or where in the source to look.
The full URL. Required.
fields.name: url
fields.title: URL

fields.description: Short display name. e.g. "Pints With Aquinas Ep 412 — John vs the Gnostics".
fields.name: label

fields.description: Why this matters, timestamp ("23:00 onward"), the snippet that caught you, what to come back to.
fields.name: note
inspirationLinks

fields.description: Your running editorial notes. Reactions to drafts, questions for me, decisions, redirects. Date your entries (e.g. "Jun 17 — pivot to John of Damascus angle").
fields.name: sheriComments
fields.title: Sheri Comments (running notes)

fields.description: My research findings, draft hooks, draft contexts, fact-check notes. I write here when working on the item; you review and respond in Sheri Comments.
fields.name: claudeNotes
fields.title: Claude Notes (research + draft material)

fields.description: Concrete next steps. Each item names an action, an owner (Sheri or Claude), a status, and an optional due date.
What needs to happen. Be specific.
fields.name: item
fields.title: Action

fields.name: actionItems

fields.description: Order in the splash sequence (1, 2, 3…). Lower numbers shown first.
fields.name: screenNumber

fields.description: A short label for this screen in Studio (e.g. "Brand Identity"). Not shown to users.
fields.name: screenTitle
fields.title: Screen Title (admin only)

fields.description: Content blocks rendered top-to-bottom. Choose one block per piece of content; visual treatment is fixed by block type.
Brand wordmark, e.g. "KALLOS". Rendered in Montserrat uppercase, wide tracking.
fields.name: text

fields.description: Pronunciation guide, e.g. "kal · os". Rendered in Cormorant italic, small.
fields.name: text

fields.description: Optional note for editors. Not displayed.
fields.name: note
fields.title: Note (admin only)

fields.description: Display quote. Rendered in Cormorant italic, large. Quotation marks are added automatically.
fields.name: text

fields.description: Screen heading. Rendered in Cormorant italic, large.
fields.name: text

fields.description: Body paragraph. Rendered in Open Sans light, line height 1.8.
fields.name: text

fields.description: Short closing line. Rendered in Montserrat verdigris (#5F7A6B), small. Optional — leave empty to keep the block as a placeholder for future text without rendering anything on the screen.
fields.name: text

fields.description: Small caps label at the top of the card (e.g. "Pause & Ponder").
fields.name: label

fields.description: One-sentence description for the card.
fields.name: body

fields.description: e.g. "Start here →". Rendered as a gold filled button.
fields.name: label
fields.title: Button Label

fields.description: App route to navigate to (e.g. /journeys/beauty-truth-and-goodness or /prompt). When tapped, marks onboarding complete.
fields.name: linkPath

fields.description: e.g. "See today's Pause & Ponder →". Rendered as a small text link.
fields.name: label
fields.title: Button Label

fields.description: App route to navigate to. When tapped, marks onboarding complete.
fields.name: linkPath
blocks

fields.description: The italic Cormorant prompt shown centered on the Visio Divina Action step. The current hardcoded value is "How will you live this out?" — used as the default for all artworks unless overridden per-content-item.
fields.name: defaultActioHeadline
fields.title: Default Actio headline

fields.description: The supporting prompt shown below the headline on the Action step. Current hardcoded value: "Ask yourself and God: How will you apply what you've received in prayer to your life?"
fields.name: defaultActioInstruction
fields.title: Default Actio instruction

fields.description: The instruction sentence above the Traditional Prayer drawer on the Pray step. Current hardcoded value: "Respond to God in prayer—thanksgiving, intercession, or simply conversation about what you notice."
fields.name: defaultPrayerPrompt
fields.title: Default Pray prompt

fields.description: The traditional prayer shown in the expandable drawer when the artwork does not have its own `traditionalPrayer` value. Replaces the previously hardcoded FALLBACK_PRAYER in PrayClient.tsx.
fields.name: defaultTraditionalPrayer

fields.description: Attribution for the default prayer above. Hidden in app when blank.
fields.name: defaultTraditionalPrayerSource
fields.title: Default Traditional Prayer — Source

fields.description: The fallback Actio shown on any P&P day where the dailyPrompt's own actio field is blank. Edit here to change the voice across every default-actio day at once. Replaces the previously hardcoded "Carry one image of beauty with you today. Let it be a question, not an answer."
